calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-26-2022 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue Sanctions, Evidence Sanctions Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Prior Order Of The Court
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR ISSUE SANCTIONS, EVIDENCE SANCTIONS, TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS.
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Roberto Santoyo (510.735.4105; marcus004@sbcglobal.net) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Warren Klein, Esq. and Christopher Tuttle, Esq. (415.777.1308; christopher.tuttle@libertymutual.com) of Law Offices of Santana, Vierra, Stevenson, Harris & Hermanson for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

The Court noted there was no compliance with contesting tentative ruling.
Discussion held, the Court determinaed no good cause to hear oral arguments and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:.

DEFENDANT FARAH ZAIDI's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue Sanctions, Evidence Sanctions Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Prior Order Of The Court

Motion for Terminating Sanctions is GRANTED as follows. No timely opposition filed. There is a history of failures to comply with discovery or this Court's orders, and there is no evidence that would show less severe sanctions would produce compliance with discovery. See Moofly Productions LLC v. Favila (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 1, 10-12 (collecting cases). Because Plaintiff did not timely respond to the requests for admissions, they are due to be deemed admitted. Having deemed the requests admitted, there appear to be no remaining triable issues. Accordingly, the complaint is ordered dismissed. CCP 2023.030(d)(3). The request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Defendant already received significant relief by virtue of dismissal.

Prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling via email to sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Brian Johnson, Esq. (415.537.2536; Brian.Johnson@libertymutual.com) of Santana Vierra Stevenson Harris & Hermanson for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FARAH ZAIDI's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Prior Order Of The Court is DENIED without prejudice.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

The court noted there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule and does not permit oral argument. Mr. Santoyo stated he had medical issues and requested continuance to allow time to obtain counsel.

DISCUSSED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FARAH ZAIDI's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories.

No written opposition filed; motion granted. Responses to be served by July 1, 2022.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling via email to davididaho14@gmail.com.
